title: Model-Based fMRI and Its Application to Reward Learning and Decision Making
venue: Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences

frameworks.name: model-based fMRI
frameworks.one_line: Correlate signals from a computational cognitive model against fMRI to localize implementation.

frameworks.name: reward learning and decision making
frameworks.one_line: Domain where computational models probe learning of reward associations and value encoding.

core_question: How can model-based fMRI reveal the neural implementation of cognitive processes underlying reward learning and decision making?
key_assumptions: A computational model of the cognitive process is available
Model-derived signals can be correlated against measured fMRI time-series
Brain regions tracking model signals implement the modeled process
manipulations: Task designed to engage reward learning or decision making
Model-derived regressors (e.g., learning signals, expected value) entered into fMRI analysis
key_findings: Model-based fMRI identifies brain regions whose response profile matches model predictions
Approach has been applied to reward associations, action selection for reward, and expected-value signals reflecting abstract decision structure
interpretations: Model-based fMRI reveals how, not merely where, a cognitive process is implemented in the brain
The approach provides mechanistic insight beyond conventional localization neuroimaging
limitations_noted: The review discusses limitations of the model-based fMRI approach
